description_long: This object consists of two intricately sculpted cherub statues, each seated on a stone base and holding a garland of flowers. The cherubs have detailed curly hair, feathered wings, and classical draped garments, evoking a serene and contemplative mood. Their expressions are gentle, and their postures are relaxed, with one leg bent and the other extended. The statues are rendered in a weathered greenish-blue patina, typical of aged bronze, and rest on a stepped stone plinth. The garland they hold is adorned with floral motifs, adding to the decorative and memorial character of the piece. This type of sculpture is commonly found as a grave marker or decorative element in historic cemeteries.
description: A pair of seated cherub statues with wings and garlands, crafted in weathered bronze atop stone bases, often used as grave markers.
description_view_0: Front view showing both cherubs facing forward, holding a garland, with detailed facial features and draped garments visible.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the depth of the statues, the curvature of the wings, and the layered stone base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the cherubs, their wings, and the folds of their garments, with the stone plinth clearly visible.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the back and side profiles of the cherubs, with the garland and wing details prominent.
